title: 10 best herbal tinctures for bronchitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea, contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its antiviral properties help to combat the underlying viral infection that often causes bronchitis. As a result, purple coneflower tinctures can provide relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness, allowing for faster recovery and reduced severity of the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate congestion and ease breathing difficulties associated with bronchitis.

Antiviral: The tincture's antiviral properties help combat the viral infections that often cause bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness.
Mucolytic: Purple coneflower's mucolytic properties help break down and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and alleviating coughing fits, congestion, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the bronchi, making it an effective treatment for bronchitis.
Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis due to their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from the wild or cultivate them in your garden carefully.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to facilitate drying and tincture production quickly.
Dry the chopped plant material in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days slowly.
Transfer the dried plant material to a clean glass jar and cover it with high-proof vodka or brandy at 1:5 ratio.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks before straining and decanting into a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The saponins present in licorice root help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for air to pass through and relieving congestion. Additionally, licorice's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from bronchitis symptoms, helping to calm a sore throat, ease a persistent cough, and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and congestion.
Mucolytic properties: Licorice root's mucolytic properties help break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving chest congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal licorice tinctures contain antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory issues.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and triterpenoids, which help to combat bacterial and viral infections associated with bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Combine one part dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with one part organic vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to extract tincture.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter and store it in a clean glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with bronchitis because its active compound, mucilage, forms a soothing film in the throat and lungs, relieving inflammation and irritation. This thick, gel-like substance also helps to protect and calm the respiratory tract, reducing coughing and congestion. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and promoting the healing process. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures can provide quick relief from bronchitis symptoms, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Mucilage production: Herbal marshmallow tinctures contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and wheezing.

Expectorant properties: The mucilage in marshmallow tinctures also helps thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and sinuses, which can help alleviate congestion and chest tightness associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which soothes and protects irritated respiratory tissues.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce bronchial inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency and quality.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1 part root to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the bioactive compounds from the roots.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and label the tincture with the date prepared and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract tissues. The herb's mucilage content forms a protective barrier that coats the lungs and airways, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by bronchitis. Mullein tincture also contains antioxidants that help eliminate free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchial constriction and inflammation. By using mullein tinctures, individuals with bronchitis may experience relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion.
Mucolytic properties: The mucolytic agents in mullein tincture break down and thin out mucus, allowing for easier expulsion from the lungs and reducing coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Mullein roots are used in tincture-making due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help relieve respiratory congestion and coughing fits associated with bronchitis.

Flowers: The flowers of the mullein plant are valued for their mucilaginous properties, which create a soothing and protective barrier in the lungs, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity.
Chop the herb into small pieces and mix with 80% to 90% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for six to eight weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use within two years taking 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato tinctures helps with bronchitis because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm inflamed airways. The herbal remedy contains compounds like solanine, which has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce coughing spasms. Additionally, the tincture's expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs, thereby relieving congestion and shortness of breath associated with bronchitis. As a natural remedy, wild potato tinctures provide a safe and effective way to alleviate bronchitis symptoms without pharmaceutical intervention.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tomatoes and potatoes contain antioxidants like vitamin C and polyphenols, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.

Mucilage production: The mucilages present in tomatoes and potatoes may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bronchitis.

Expectorant properties: Some species within the Solanum family, like Solanum xanthocarpum, have been traditionally used as expectorants to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, which can help relieve congestion and coughs associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and nutrients, roots help to soothe coughs and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem system of the wild potato plant, rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in relieving bronchial congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphorbia esculenta tubers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency and quality.
Wash the tubers gently with cold distilled water to remove any dirt or debris before drying them thoroughly.
Chop the dried tubers into small pieces and soak them in 80% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol for six hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Store the herbal tincture in an amber-colored glass bottle in a cool dark place for at least two weeks before use to allow maturation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The tincture's expectorant qualities also help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, dandelion's natural antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection causing bronchitis, reducing congestion and coughing. By supporting lung health and promoting effective expulsion of mucus, dandelion tinctures provide a natural and holistic approach to managing bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can alleviate bronchitis symptoms like coughing and wheezing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tincture, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing bronchial irritation.

Mucilaginous properties: Dandelion tincture's mucilaginous compounds, like pectin, can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, reducing inflammation and coughing, while also supporting the body's natural mucus-clearing processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated bronchial passages.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots taraxacum officinale or use dried roots from the local market.
Dry the fresh dandelion roots in a warm place or with a food dehydrator for 6 hours.
Chop the dried dandelion roots into small pieces and combine with 60 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily for 5 minutes each day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids transferring to amber glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal extract calms irritated airways, reducing inflammation and constriction, which alleviates coughing and congestion. Its antimicrobial compounds also help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate bronchitis. By reducing swelling and suppressing the growth of harmful microorganisms, aloe tinctures provide natural relief from bronchial discomfort, making it an effective adjunct to traditional treatments for managing bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Mucolytic properties: The mucilages present in aloe vera tincture break down thick mucus and phlegm, allowing for easier expulsion from the lungs and relieving congestion.

Antimicrobial properties: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as salicylic acid, help combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms, reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to relieve bronchial congestion and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and slice them into thin pieces for extraction.
Combine the sliced aloe vera with vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:3 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks allowing the mixture to steep slowly.
Strain the liquid from the aloe vera solids using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label with date and contents at a dosage of 20-30 drops 3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with bronchitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, also have expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier clearance. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help to combat infections that may be exacerbating bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in ginger tincture help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion and chest tightness.

Antioxidant and antiviral activity: Ginger's antioxidant and antiviral properties may help combat bronchitis-causing pathogens, such as viruses and bacteria, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, ginger roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving bronchial congestion and coughs.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the thick, edible portion of the ginger plant that contains a concentrated amount of active compounds, including gingerol and shogaol, which help to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots for processing weighing at least one pound.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm oven set to low temperature or using desiccant method taking several days.
Chop the dried ginger root into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka filling a clean glass jar completely.
Allow the mixture to steep for six weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction then straining through cheesecloth.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles storing them in a cool dark place and labeling the contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with bronchitis because it contains a high concentration of catechin, a powerful antioxidant that soothes inflamed airways and reduces congestion. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm coughing spasms, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for better oxygenation and reduced respiratory distress. By addressing the underlying inflammation and congestion, Jesuit's bark tinctures provides natural relief from bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cinchona Alkaloids: These alkaloids, particularly quinine and quinidine, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to relieve bronchial constriction and reduce inflammation in the airways.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids present in Jesuit's Bark have been shown to exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, reducing bronchial spasms and improving breathing.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks contain quinine, a natural antispasmodic that helps relieve respiratory spasms and inflammation associated with bronchitis.
Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated airways and reduce bronchial constriction in individuals with bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark from sustainably sourced trees for optimal potency and quality.
Clean the harvested bark by sorting through it to remove impurities and debris.
Dry the cleaned bark in a controlled environment at 35-40°c for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and store it in an airtight container.
Mix 1 part powdered cinchona officinalis with 2 parts 95% ethanol and let it macerate for 14 days before straining and filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the airways and reduce swelling in the lungs. The herbal extract also possesses expectorant qualities, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the chest, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. Additionally, butterbur's ability to calm spasms in the bronchial tubes eases coughing fits and makes it easier to breathe, providing welcome relief to those suffering from bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butterbur tincture contains compounds like petasins, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, alleviating bronchitis symptoms.

Antispasmotic: The flavonoids present in butterbur tincture help relax smooth muscle spasms in the airways, relieving bronchospasms and making it easier to breathe during an acute episode of bronchitis.
Mucolytic: Butterbur's mucolytic properties help break down excess mucus and facilitate its clearance from the lungs, reducing congestion and coughing associated with bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the butterbur plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making them effective in soothing bronchial spasms and relieving bronchitis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of butterbur have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, providing relief from bronchitis-related coughing and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petasites hybridus leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop 200g of fresh herbs into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife.
Combine chopped herbs with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding solids and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle tinctures helps with bronchitis because it contains compounds that effectively soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing coughing and congestion. The tincture's antiviral properties also help to combat the underlying infection causing bronchitis, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, its expectorant qualities aid in clearing mucus from the lungs, making breathing easier and more comfortable. As a result, Japanese honeysuckle tinctures have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for centuries to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Japanese Honeysuckle contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause bronchitis, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the infection.

Expectorant properties: The tincture's saponins and flavonoids help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion, which is a common symptom of bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Japanese Honeysuckle contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm respiratory issues, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Rhyzomes: Contains pungent and bitter principles that stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lonicera japonica flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried off.
Cleanly chop 4 ounces of freshly harvested material into small pieces to ensure proper extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it to about an inch from the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids and transferring liquid tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with bronchitis because it possesses potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that effectively combat bacterial and viral infections, reducing inflammation in the airways. The herb's citral content also relaxes respiratory muscles, easing coughing and congestion. Additionally, lemongrass has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. As a result, herbal lemongrass tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from bronchitis symptoms, promoting a smoother breathing experience and faster recovery time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, which helps alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as wheezing and chest tightness.
Antimicrobial property: Lemongrass tincture exhibits antimicrobial activity against bacteria and viruses that can cause bronchitis, helping to combat infections and promote healing.

Expectorant property: The citral content in lemongrass tincture acts as a natural expectorant, loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemongrass plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, making them a popular choice for creating medicinal tinctures for bronchitis due to their expectorant properties.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain oils that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tinctures for bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Cleanse and dry the harvested leaves to remove impurities and excess moisture using a clean cloth.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Steep 1 part of chopped cymbopogon citratus in 2 parts of high-proof vodka or ethanol at room temperature for 6 hours.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store for 6 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its natural antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The herbal extract soothes inflamed airways, relaxing constricted bronchial muscles to ease wheezing and shortness of breath. Rosemary's ability to thin mucus also helps to expel it from the lungs, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect lung tissue from further damage, promoting healthy healing and recovery from bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and wheezing.
Expectorant properties: Rosemary's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion associated with bronchitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as chest tightness and difficulty breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with bronchitis.
Barks: The barks of the rosemary plant contain compounds that help to relax the respiratory tract muscles, which can alleviate breathing difficulties and coughing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers.
Combine one part of the harvested plant material with four parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the herbal extract to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Label and store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years taking 20-30 drops twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve coughing spasms. The Boswellia resin present in frankincense tinctures has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing swelling and congestion in the lungs and bronchial tubes. Additionally, frankincense's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and aiding in the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense tinctures contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, helping to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and congestion.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in frankincense tinctures help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms and slow down healing.
Expectorant properties: Frankincense tinctures have a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain a high concentration of resin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe irritated airways.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of frankincense have been traditionally used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia serrata roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and soak them in a mixture of ethanol and glycerin for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 7 days.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 6 months to allow it to mature.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter before using it to treat bronchitis symptoms such as coughing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with bronchitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The curcuminoids present in turmeric inhibit the production of prostaglandins, known to cause constriction of airways and increase mucus production. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that can exacerbate bronchitis symptoms. By reducing swelling and fighting off underlying infections, herbal turmeric tinctures provide relief from coughing, wheezing, and chest congestion associated with bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's main active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs and airways associated with bronchitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial and viral pathogens that can cause bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae and influenza virus, helping to combat infection and promote healing.

Expectorant properties: The bioactive compounds in turmeric tincture, particularly curcuminoids, have been found to stimulate the production of mucus and thin its consistency, making it easier to cough up and clear from the lungs, thereby relieving bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are the underground stems of the turmeric plant, contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe respiratory inflammation associated with bronchitis.

Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in bioactive compounds like volatile oils and polyphenols, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis by reducing cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa rhizomes in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at low heat.
Grind 200g of dried rhizomes into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground rhizome powder with 1l of 80% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring full immersion for 14 days.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with bronchitis because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion. The tincture's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, fennel's antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing coughing fits and wheezing. By addressing these underlying symptoms, herbal fennel tinctures can provide relief from bronchitis-related discomfort and help speed up recovery.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion in the chest.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, which can help to ease coughing and difficulty breathing associated with bronchitis.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic properties relax and calm the smooth muscle lining the airways, reducing spasms that can cause wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and antioxidants, fennel roots help soothe coughs and reduce inflammation associated with bronchitis.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a volatile oil that has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and store them in an airtight container.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with one part high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the foeniculum vulgare tincture.
Transfer the foeniculum vulgare tincture to glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator at 2-3 degrees celsius for up to two years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for bronchitis are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, which have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of bronchial inflammation. These tinctures help by soothing irritated airways, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus, making it easier to breathe. Examples of effective herbal tinctures for bronchitis include Thyme, Echinacea, and Wild Indigo, which have been shown to reduce coughing, congestion, and fatigue. By using these natural remedies, individuals with bronchitis can experience improved lung function, reduced reliance on pharmaceuticals, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to return to their daily activities with greater ease and confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bronchitis with herbal tinctures is to combine a few specific tinctures in a synergistic blend. Combine Echinacea angustifolia, which reduces inflammation, with Thyme, which has antimicrobial properties, and Ginger, which soothes the respiratory tract. Take 20-30 drops of the blend three times daily, diluted in water or juice. Additionally, drink plenty of warm liquids and rest to aid recovery.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bronchitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are chronic coughs, asthma, and COPD. Herbs such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease breathing. Tinctures of these herbs can be used in combination with other natural remedies to alleviate symptoms and promote relaxation. Additionally, tinctures of ginger and turmeric may also be beneficial for reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory discomfort.
